resolution_strategies: Insert the missing activity "legitimiation of suspected customer" with resource "money laundering officer" in the existing new-customer sequence, immediately after "conduct customer identification & legitimation" and before "conclude custody account". This is the most localized fix for R5, preserves the existing precedence of "conclude custody account" after customer identification, and does not affect "conduct risk disclosure", "conduct investment advice", or "update customer data".

In the new-customer branch, insert an exclusive choice immediately after "conduct customer identification & legitimation": one branch with condition "data.customer_status == \"untrusted\"" containing "legitimiation of suspected customer" executed by "money laundering officer", and one empty branch with condition "not data.customer_status == \"untrusted\"". Continue afterwards with "conclude custody account". This avoids executing the suspected-customer legitimation for all customers while still ensuring it occurs for untrusted customers.

Replace the current activity "conduct customer identification & legitimation" in the new-customer branch with a sequence containing "conduct customer identification & legitimation" executed by "customer advisor" followed by "legitimiation of suspected customer" executed by "money laundering officer", then continue with "conclude custody account". This separates the customer-advisor identification task from the money-laundering-officer suspected-customer legitimation task while preserving the required order before account conclusion.

Replace the current activity "conduct customer identification & legitimation" in the new-customer branch with a parallel block containing two branches: one branch with "conduct customer identification & legitimation" executed by "customer advisor" and the other branch with "legitimiation of suspected customer" executed by "money laundering officer". After the parallel block, continue with "conclude custody account". This satisfies the missing executed-by requirement while keeping both checks before account conclusion, but it is less conservative than a sequential insertion because it changes the execution relation between the two legitimation activities.
